description:
Goal
Introduce the compiler2 return-evidence payload and replace the activation return fact boundary with it in one coherent commit. This is the data-model move: absence remains fact absence; produced evidence becomes a named semantic value.

Signal
- New tests cover the three distinct produced states: known return type, explicit dynamic/unknown boundary evidence, and no-return control flow.
- `ActivationSlot` no longer stores `Option<Ty>` for returns; it stores `Option<ReturnEvidence>` or an equivalent explicit payload.
- `FactKey::ReturnType(ActivationKey)` is renamed or replaced at the scheduler boundary; telemetry carries return evidence, not a bare `return_ty` pretending to prove readiness.
- Cyclic activation convergence joins/widens only the known-type component of evidence; non-cyclic activations still replace stale evidence.
- No compatibility API returns a `Ty` merely to preserve the old world view.

Tactics
- Start in `src/compiler2/semantic.rs`, `src/compiler2/drive.rs`, and `src/compiler2/world.rs`.
- Update the focused semantic/activation-cycle tests in their `_test.rs` files.
- Keep `any`/`none` constructors out of the evidence model except as explicit known domain values.

Outro
No `ReturnType` fact name, no `return_type.defined` event as a bare type publication, no `ActivationSlot { return_ty: Option<Ty> }`, and no helper whose purpose is “make ReturnEvidence look like old ReturnType”.
title: Compiler2 return evidence: replace the activation return fact payload
